name: Service Mesh for Microservices
strategicIntent: Implement a service mesh using the sidecar proxy model to provide consistent observability, traffic management, and mutual TLS between microservices across McCain's containerized workloads.
problemStatement: As McCain's microservices estate grows, teams implement inconsistent approaches to service discovery, retries, circuit-breaking, and inter-service encryption, leading to reliability gaps and difficulty diagnosing cross-service failures in production.

useCases.use: AKS-hosted microservices requiring mutual TLS and traffic shaping
useCases.dontUse: Monolithic applications running on VMs with no containerization plan

useCases.use: Cross-namespace service communication needing fine-grained access policies
useCases.dontUse: Simple two-tier web applications with a single backend service

useCases.use: Canary and blue-green deployments requiring traffic splitting
useCases.dontUse: Batch processing jobs with no real-time inter-service communication

components: Istio Service Mesh — control plane for traffic management and policy
Envoy Sidecar Proxy — data plane for request routing and telemetry
Jaeger — distributed tracing for cross-service request flows
Prometheus & Grafana — metrics collection and visualization
interactionSteps: Envoy sidecar is automatically injected into each pod via Istio admission webhook
Service-to-service calls are intercepted by Envoy and encrypted with mutual TLS
Istio VirtualService rules manage traffic splitting for canary deployments
DestinationRules configure circuit-breaking thresholds and retry policies
Jaeger captures distributed traces across the full request chain
Kiali dashboard visualizes service topology, traffic flow, and error rates
Prometheus scrapes Envoy metrics and Grafana renders operational dashboards
securityConsiderations: Mutual TLS must be enforced in STRICT mode for all mesh-enrolled namespaces
Istio AuthorizationPolicies must restrict cross-namespace access to explicit allow-lists
Sidecar resource limits must be configured to prevent proxy resource exhaustion
Control plane components must run in a dedicated, hardened namespace
Mesh telemetry data must not contain PII or sensitive payload content

guardrails.rule: mTLS STRICT mode required for all production namespaces
Circuit-breaker thresholds must be configured for all inter-service calls
Distributed tracing must be enabled for all mesh services
Sidecar CPU/memory limits must be set per workload tier

exceptionProcess: Submit Platform Engineering variance request for non-mesh service communication. Must include network-level compensating controls (NSG rules, private endpoints) and security team sign-off.

revisionHistory.author: Carlos Mendes
revisionHistory.summary: Initial service mesh pattern with Istio on AKS

revisionHistory.author: Carlos Mendes
revisionHistory.summary: Added canary deployment support, circuit-breaker guardrails, and Kiali integration
